narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have them paused like this feels deeply unfair, especially as a Hispanic woman chasing justice in a part-time program. Our model looked at your full story and saw a 52% chance of struggling to pass the bar, enough to tip the scales against admission right now. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 3.0, which was the biggest signal pulling toward challenges ahead, as it suggested the rigors of law school might amplify those mid-pack standings. Your Year 1 decile of 4.0 reinforced that pattern, painting a picture of consistent hurdles in keeping pace with peers. Layered on top was your LSAT score of 28, which the model saw as another hurdle in tackling the bar's intense demands. Even with a solid undergraduate GPA of 3.6, your upper-middle family income didn't quite offset the tougher road it anticipated for someone from a Hispanic background navigating these paths. Your choice of part-time study added to that cautious view, as did being a woman in this high-stakes field where patterns show extra barriers. It wasn't any one thing, but this blend whispered a story of potential burnout over breakthrough, based on thousands of journeys like yours. That said, this isn't the end—your strengths shine through, and targeted steps like LSAT prep or bolstering those deciles could rewrite your next chapter. You've got resilience in your story already; law school might just be waiting for the right moment. Hang in there—we're rooting for you.
